name: done-when-pipeline
description: Done_When Pipeline — turn fuzzy natural-language requirements into machine-verifiable completion contracts, then run a multi-agent acceptance loop against an implementation. Three skills cover Steps 1-6: `acceptance-spec` (Steps 1-3: NL → EARS spec + done_when.yaml via strict 3-question-type clarify loop + S2.5 spec-self-adversarial pass that emits spec-robustness.md identifying gaming vectors per the 6 RHD patterns — 5-file output), `test-suite-generator` (Step 4: EARS → full test pyramid with existence/unit/integration/e2e tests + mutation-testing anti-reward-hacking config + fitness rubrics), and `acceptance-fleet` (Steps 5-6: 7-role evaluation fleet + standalone spec-gaming-detector + non-debating meta-judge synthesis + four-state ratchet DONE/FIX/SPEC_DRIFT/GAMING_RISK with full ratchet-log/iteration-NNN trace; cross-vendor evaluators preferred — Codex/Gemini break the Claude-reviewing-Claude sycophancy loop). The /ratchet hand-off remains supported as a legacy lighter path. Anti-gaming structural guarantee: implementation agents MUST NOT see evaluator prompts; minimum medium isolation (mixed Claude sizes) enforced. Borrows file formats from OpenSpec, clarify protocol from GitHub Spec Kit, role design from Anthropic Code Review + BMAD edge-case-hunter + Builder.io QRA, all without CLI integration. Design philosophy: verifiable beats judgeable; human time only spent on irreplaceable ambiguity removal; debate amplifies bias (use meta-judge synthesis, not multi-agent debate); PBT + mutation testing + cross-vendor review close the reward-hacking loop.
